description:
You are a script developer setting up a small collection of helper utilities under
/home/user/utilities.
Before writing any new code, you first need to confirm — and permanently record —
that the interactive shell (the “bash” package) really is installed on the
system.  For reproducibility, you want the log to contain exactly the same line
that the dpkg package manager shows for the *installed* (“ii …”) record of
bash.

Goal
1. Use the Debian/Ubuntu package-management toolchain (i.e. the *dpkg –l*
   listing) to obtain the single line that starts with the status code
   “ii”, followed by two spaces and the word “bash”.
   Example layout of the desired line (your exact version and date will differ):
        ii  bash  5.2.15-2+b2  amd64  GNU Bourne Again SHell
2. Redirect that single line, and **only** that one line, into the file
   /home/user/utilities/bash-package.log.
   • The file must end with a single “\n”.
   • There must be no blank lines before or after the record.
3. When you are finished, the directory and file structure must look like:
   /home/user/utilities/                  (directory, already or newly created)
   └── bash-package.log                   (regular text file as described above)

Automated test‐suite checks
• /home/user/utilities/bash-package.log exists.  
• The file contains exactly one line.  
• That single line begins with the nine-character prefix  
  “ii␣␣bash␣” (two spaces after “ii”).  
• No other content appears before or after that line.

Hints
• The prefix “ii” in a dpkg listing denotes an **installed** package.
• Make sure you do **not** capture the column headers, nor the
  “Desired/Status/Err?” explanatory text.
• You only need a single terminal command (plus any redirection you find
  convenient) to fulfil the requirement; no root privileges are necessary.
truth:
expected_file:/home/user/utilities/bash-package.log
expected_line_prefix:ii␣␣bash␣
expected_line_count:1
# The automated verifier will:
#   1. read /home/user/utilities/bash-package.log
#   2. assert there is exactly one line
#   3. assert that line starts with expected_line_prefix
#      (“␣” represents the literal ASCII space 0x20)
name: task_000000_b8db35d7
